schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

schemas.name: Financial Modelling for Startups | Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: Investor-ready financial models and pitch deck support. Revenue projections, unit economics, scenario analysis, cap table modelling. For fundraising startups. Virtual Auditor.

mainEntity.name: What is included in a financial model?

acceptedAnswer.text: Revenue projections (3-5 years), P&L, balance sheet, cash flow, unit economics (CAC, LTV, payback), break-even analysis, scenario analysis (3 cases), sensitivity tables, and key assumptions documentation.

mainEntity.name: Do you help with pitch decks?

acceptedAnswer.text: We provide the financial content for pitch decks: market sizing, revenue model, unit economics, financial projections, and use-of-funds breakdown. We do not design slides but provide the data and narrative that goes into them.
Standard: 5-7 working days. Express: 3 days. Depends on complexity of the business model and number of revenue streams.

mainEntity.name: What does a financial model include?

acceptedAnswer.text: 5-year integrated financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow), revenue model with unit economics, cost structure, working capital, capex schedule, debt schedule, and scenario analysis (base, bull, bear).

mainEntity.name: How is a financial model different from a valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Financial model: forward-looking projections of business performance. Valuation: determination of company worth using those projections (and other methods). Financial model is INPUT to DCF valuation.

mainEntity.name: Do you build models for fundraising?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Investor-ready models with: revenue ramp (Bayesian), unit economics dashboard (CAC, LTV, payback), cohort analysis, sensitivity tables, and automatic dilution calculation for different funding scenarios.

mainEntity.name: What tools do you use for financial modelling?

acceptedAnswer.text: Advanced Excel with VBA automation. Python for Monte Carlo simulations. Our proprietary Valuation Engine Pro for automated sensitivity and scenario analysis. Delivered as interactive Excel workbook.

mainEntity.name: How much does a financial model cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Basic 3-statement model: ₹25,000-₹40,000. Investor-ready model with scenarios: ₹50,000-₹75,000. Full model + valuation package: ₹75,000-₹1,50,000. Includes 2 rounds of revisions.
